CHANAKYAIQ DOCUMENTATION STRUCTURE
=====================================

Created: June 13, 2026
Purpose: Complete project context for developers and AI agents

FILES CREATED
-------------
1. README.md              - Documentation index and navigation hub
2. AGENTS.md              - Complete project documentation (PRIMARY REFERENCE)
3. OAUTH2-FLOW.md         - Detailed authentication flow with diagrams
4. DOCUMENTATION-MAP.md   - Visual guide to documentation structure

DOCUMENTATION PHILOSOPHY
------------------------
- Single source of truth: AGENTS.md contains all core information
- Specialized deep dives: Separate files for complex topics (OAuth2)
- Bi-directional linking: Easy navigation between related topics
- Progressive disclosure: README → AGENTS.md → Specialized docs
- No duplication: Cross-reference instead of copying content

KEY CROSS-REFERENCES
--------------------
AGENTS.md → OAUTH2-FLOW.md (3 links):
  - Header section (line 3)
  - Authentication Flow section (line ~145)
  - Security Configuration section (line ~310)

OAUTH2-FLOW.md → AGENTS.md (1 link):
  - Header section (line 3)

CONTENT BREAKDOWN
-----------------

AGENTS.md (~500 lines):
  ✓ Project overview and purpose
  ✓ Complete tech stack (Java 21, Spring Boot 4, React 19, TypeScript)
  ✓ Architecture (layered, interface-driven)
  ✓ Directory structure with explanations
  ✓ Coding standards (Records, Lombok, TypeScript)
  ✓ Key features (auth, portfolio, trading, market simulation)
  ✓ Complete API endpoint reference
  ✓ Database schema (Users, Holdings, Transactions)
  ✓ Configuration files (application.properties, .env, package.json)
  ✓ Development workflow (running, building, testing)
  ✓ Security configuration (CORS, OAuth2, sessions)
  ✓ Business logic deep dives (trade execution, portfolio calc, market sim)
  ✓ UI/UX design system
  ✓ Common development tasks
  ✓ Testing setup
  ✓ Known limitations and future enhancements
  ✓ Troubleshooting guide
  ✓ Git repository info
  ✓ Documentation index with quick reference table

OAUTH2-FLOW.md (~450 lines):
  ✓ Complete OAuth2 flow diagram (step-by-step)
  ✓ Key components explained (frontend, backend, Google)
  ✓ OAuth2 data flow (tokens, claims, user info)
  ✓ Session management (JSESSIONID cookies)
  ✓ User provisioning logic
  ✓ Security features (HttpOnly, CORS, CSRF)
  ✓ Configuration checklist
  ✓ Testing procedures
  ✓ Common issues and solutions
  ✓ Flow summary table

README.md (~100 lines):
  ✓ Documentation overview
  ✓ Quick navigation table
  ✓ Project structure
  ✓ Quick start guide
  ✓ Key technologies
  ✓ Documentation philosophy
  ✓ Contribution guidelines

DOCUMENTATION-MAP.md (~250 lines):
  ✓ Visual hierarchy diagram
  ✓ When to use each document
  ✓ Cross-reference map
  ✓ Documentation flow for common tasks
  ✓ Quick links by topic table
  ✓ Maintenance guidelines

USAGE PATTERNS
--------------
For AI Agents:
  1. Start with AGENTS.md for complete context
  2. Reference OAUTH2-FLOW.md for authentication tasks
  3. Use cross-references to navigate between topics
  4. Refer to code examples in both documents

For Developers:
  1. Begin with README.md for orientation
  2. Read AGENTS.md sections relevant to your task
  3. Deep dive into OAUTH2-FLOW.md if working on auth
  4. Use DOCUMENTATION-MAP.md to find specific topics

For New Team Members:
  1. README.md → Overview
  2. AGENTS.md → Complete read-through
  3. OAUTH2-FLOW.md → Understand authentication
  4. AGENTS.md → Development Workflow → Start coding

MAINTENANCE GUIDELINES
----------------------
Update AGENTS.md when:
  - Adding new features
  - Changing architecture
  - Adding API endpoints
  - Modifying database schema
  - Updating configuration

Update OAUTH2-FLOW.md when:
  - Changing authentication flow
  - Modifying security config
  - Adding OAuth providers
  - Updating session management

Update README.md when:
  - Adding new documentation files
  - Changing documentation structure

Update DOCUMENTATION-MAP.md when:
  - Adding cross-references
  - Restructuring documents

KEY FEATURES DOCUMENTED
-----------------------
✓ Google OAuth2 authentication (session-based)
✓ Auto-provisioning users with ₹10L balance
✓ Portfolio management with real-time P&L
✓ Mock market data simulation (IST hours)
✓ Buy/sell trading with weighted average pricing
✓ Transaction history
✓ H2 database (file-based)
✓ CORS configuration for cross-origin requests
✓ Session cookie management (JSESSIONID)
✓ Real-time price updates (5-second polling)

TECHNOLOGY STACK
----------------
Backend:
  - Java 21
  - Spring Boot 4.0.6
  - Spring Security + OAuth2
  - JPA/Hibernate
  - H2 Database
  - Maven
  - Lombok

Frontend:
  - React 19.2.6
  - TypeScript 6.0.3
  - Vite 8.0.12
  - Custom CSS (dark glass-morphism)

Development:
  - Backend: localhost:8080
  - Frontend: localhost:5173
  - Database Console: localhost:8080/h2-console

---------------------
Total Files: 4

Cross-References: 4
Code Examples: 20+
Diagrams: 1 (ASCII flow diagram)
Tables: 8
Quick Reference Links: 30+

QUALITY METRICS
---------------
- Context completeness: ✓ High (can understand project without exploring code)
- Navigation efficiency: ✓ High (cross-referenced, indexed)
- Maintenance burden: ✓ Low (no duplication, clear ownership)
- AI agent readability: ✓ High (structured, comprehensive)
- Developer onboarding: ✓ High (progressive disclosure)

END OF SUMMARY
